programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nigel becomes manager of the team. Roy leaves after Pat refuses to go on a cruise with him. Sharon gives Grant a letter from the solicitors

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark and Ruth have their hen and stag parties, leaving Sharon home alone. Mark gets drunk and has a heart to heart with Arthur, and tells Ruth he has no doubts about their wedding

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Borough Commander Adam Okaro drops in and discovers all is not well in Sun Hill, with Gina and John at loggerheads over the zero-tolerance policy and the bad press it is generating. Kezia sets up a sting to get a confession from Beth Adamson's stalker Keith Durante, but the case takes a sinister turn when the frightened woman disappears under the nose of her plain-clothes police protector

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac tries to pass her message to Joseph but drifts in and out of consciousness after her accident. A mix-up ensues when Elliot is left recalling memories of Gina when he invites a celebrity friend by the name of Gordon Ramsey to the relaunch of the charity and the visitor's difficulties are greater than being mistaken for a TV chef

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tally and Beth are involved in a serious car accident while Harry is out having a secret dinner with Selina. Jane turns to Simon for support when a scan reveals she has a tumour, and a patient's death prompts Merlin to search for his real mum. Hospital drama, starring Simon MacCorkindale, Ashlie Walker and N'Deaye Baa-Clements

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Stalker Keith Durante is finally brought in for questioning after Kezia leans on his wife to retract the alibi she gave him. However, Jack Meadows finds the interrogation a frustrating affair and resorts to devious methods to extract a confession. A fire in a B&B alerts the Sun Hill watch to an illegal immigration operation on their patch, and Sam finally accepts she has no future with Stuart

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nigel becomes manager of the team. Roy leaves after Pat refuses to go on a cruise with him. Sharon gives Grant a letter from the solicitors

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark and Ruth have their hen and stag parties, leaving Sharon home alone. Mark gets drunk and has a heart to heart with Arthur, and tells Ruth he has no doubts about their wedding

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: McKenna becomes obsessed with the wife of a wounded war veteran he is treating, while Finlay and Cameron are intrigued by a film star staying in a local hotel. Rural medical drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Dawn's son slips into a coma, leaving surgeons in a state of confusion as to what could have brought about his rapid deterioration. It's not long before estranged father Keith turns up on the scene, adding to the air of tension as his wife's involvement with Andrew comes to light. Medical drama, starring Gary Mavers, Haydn Gwynne and Sarah Parish

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James realises he has a rival for Helen's affections, while Siegfried reaches breaking point with Miss Harbottle. Tristan has another all-nighter with the bell ringers, leading Siegfried to believe he has too much time on his hands - so he puts him in charge of some newly acquired piglets

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Emma asks her parents to babysit while she and Peter attend a function in London. Meanwhile, Hester delivers meals on wheels to one of Sonia's old ladies but gets lost, collides with a policeman and causes a traffic jam. comedy, starring Julia MaKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom's attempts to entertain his two obnoxious grandchildren prove a washout in more ways than one - so dauntless Diana decides to teach the two underage delinquents a lesson they'll never forget. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: A medal for bravery, a suicide pill and an unlikely hiding place all add up to a confusing time for reluctant resistance fighter Rene, who determines to flee to England with Yvette instead of the two British airmen Fairfax and Carstairs

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Tom reckons he can turn Babs into a musical star so Clegg and Truly offer to collect some music for him - only to discover the 'music' is actually a large console organ. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Tom Owen

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: The beleaguered barrister puts aside his domestic problems to defend a prostitute on the charge of murdering a high-profile businessman - a tricky case made all the more difficult by the discovery that she has many enemies, all of them determined to blacken her character. Drama, starring John Thaw

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra reopens an investigation into the suspected suicide of a Foreign Office diplomat, whose body was found in a frozen London lake after she had suffered the personal tragedy of a miscarriage and the professional embarrassment of having a government laptop stolen from her home. The woman's fiance and twin sister both believe her death is connected to sensitive information held on the missing computer and blame her boss for her downfall - and when Sandra clashes with the man himself, it only makes her more determined to pursue the conspiracy theory, despite Strickland's advice to tread carefully. Simon Day and Sharon Small guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Harry activates sleeper agent Fred Roberts, who had previously been living as a renowned chemist, and uses him to infiltrate a terrorist group. However, as Fred plays his part in the elaborate sting operation, Tom begins to question the morality of MI5's actions. Meanwhile, an encounter with photographer Will North has unexpected repercussions for Zoe. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When a jockey dies at the racecourse, the detectives find themselves on the sinister side of the sport after discovering he was poisoned with a blood-thinning drug. Their investigations lead them to links with a stable hand who has been missing for over a year, a gambling lottery winner, who happens to be the racecourse's biggest client, and a mysterious vet

A fire in a B&B alerts the Sun Hill watch to an illegal immigration operation on their patch, and Sam finally accepts she has no future with Stuart

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Harry returns to work, much to the concern of the staff who feel he cannot possibly cope so soon after his wife's death, and Simon lands himself in trouble when he keeps disappearing from A&E to observe Jane's operation. An attack on Colette makes her fear she might lose the baby and prompts her to tell Josh she's pregnant

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will returns to duty after recovering from injuries sustained in the explosion and immediately becomes a hero when he foils a suicide bid by long-suffering Louisa Durante. His moment of glory is tinged with disappointment on learning Emma is engaged to Matt, and he later discovers Dan is using steroids to boost his boxing prospects. Aware of the pressure on veterans Tony and Reg, Smithy gives them what seems to be a simple dog-napping case, but it's not long before Sun Hill has a murder inquiry on its hands

programme.desc: Dr Cameron agrees to help trace Millie Calm's real mother, but soon wishes he had never got involved. Rural medical drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: New boy Sam Morgan breaks all the rules and gets emotionally involved with a patient, jeopardising his career at the Beeches almost before it's begun. Joseph Millson stars, with Gary Mavers and Haydn Gwynne

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James takes Helen to a posh restaurant as his attempts to woo her continue, but when things don't quite go to plan, Tristan tries to brighten up James's love life by arranging a double blind date

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: The couple try to get slim _ but Hester's diet plans do not appeal to William's taste, especially when alcohol is also restricted. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana grow bored with their cosy lifestyles, and decide to look for jobs. Meanwhile, Bayview boss Harvey comes under attack for not providing residents with adequate heating. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden as the retirement home delinquents

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene and Edith are rewarded with medals by Churchill after being transported to England in place of the British airmen, and are reunited with an old friend who has changed sides

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Tom's niece disappears following his bid to launch her into showbiz stardom at a pub. Luckily, help is close at hand as Barry, Clegg and Truly begin a desperate search resulting in a surprising reward for finding her

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: The detective is called to a remote farmhouse, where he finds an elderly man has been murdered, and his wife is close to death. He hears the woman mutter something before dying, but cannot decipher her words. When he is pressured into revealing what he believes they were, the consequences of his reply have a serious impact on life in the town of Ystad. Drama based on the best-selling books by Henning Mankell, starring Kenneth Branagh, Sarah Smart and Richard McCabe, with Dearbhla Molloy and June Watson

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Retired detective Steve McAndrew arrives from Glasgow to help reinvestigate one of his original cases when a missing girl's DNA is found at the scene of a petrol station robbery in Clapham, south London. It's clear that the inquiry is a very personal one for McAndrew, and although his personality rubs the team up the wrong way, his methods soon pay dividends. Denis Lawson stars

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Danny and the team investigate the attempted assassination of a famous novelist, and begin to suspect he may not have been the intended target. The evidence they uncover suggests a senior member of the Government has made a deal that threatens to undermine UK security and the life of an innocent man. Guest starring Tim McInnerny

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective duo are called upon after a man kills his wife in a precise imitation of an illusionist's act on a TV show. When the mutilated body of another woman involved with the couple's solicitor is found with a mysterious symbol carved into her skin, Dalziel and Pascoe find themselves being drawn into the world of the paranormal. Richard E Grant guest stars, with Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will returns to duty after recovering from injuries sustained in the explosion and immediately becomes a hero when he foils a suicide bid by long-suffering Louisa Durante. His moment of glory is tinged with disappointment on learning Emma is engaged to Matt, and he later discovers Dan is using steroids to boost his boxing prospects. Aware of the pressure on veterans Tony and Reg, Smithy gives them what seems to be a simple dog-napping case, but it's not long before Sun Hill has a murder inquiry on its hands

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jack is taken into custody after Bex spots him visiting Tony and maliciously reports his whereabouts to the police. Simon continues to visit Jane but her operation has left her paralysed on one side, and Harry is painfully reminded of his wife's death when he fails to resuscitate a patient, prompting some much-needed comfort from Lara. Charlie offers Duffy a place to stay and Merlin receives a letter telling him his mother has been found

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Hospital-grade morphine appears on the streets of Sun Hill and is soon traced back to St Hugh's, where the doctor in charge of the internal investigation proves less than cooperative with the police. Under pressure from Will, Dan admits his debt to illegal steroid use and agrees to throw his big fight, but gets carried away with the atmosphere and knocks his opponent out. Tony pays his last respects as his father passes away peacefully

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The wedding bells are ringing, but Michelle's having trouble getting to the church. Arthur makes his speech, but Ruth finds her smiles are mixed with tears on her big day

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nigel has bad news for Arthur, Michelle is having difficulty living with a honeymoon couple, Grant and Sharon sort things out at the Vic, and Phil visits Ian

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Family secrets are revealed when Dr Finlay's mother comes to stay at Arden House. Dr Jennifer Napier, now using her own name, treats Annie Kirk who is suffering from a heart condition. Rural medical drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Sam makes an unusual bet with a smoker anxious to quit, and Joanna confides in pub landlady Rita, while Andrew tries to help a farmer whose failing health has prompted him to run amok in Cardale, leaving a trail of devastation behind him. Joseph Millson, Haydn Gwynne and Gary Mavers star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James manages to arrange a date with Helen after she visits the surgery with her dog Dan, while Tristan runs into problems with an awkward customer and Siegfried's temper fails to improve when he tries to give up smoking

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester makes plans to stand for election to the local residents' association and enlists William to help with her speech. However, she soon finds her success jeopardised by an attractive rival. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Harvey panders to a new guest who claims to be royalty and gets engaged at a hunt ball - much to the distress of his adoring assistant Jane

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: General Von Klinkerhoffen, tiring of the amount of Resistance propaganda in town, decides to restart the local newspaper, and appoints Rene and Alphonse to run it

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo's son Tom settles into his father's old house, but soon runs into trouble when he leaves something awkwardly personal in Barry's garage. Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Tom Owen star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest spies trouble when Lady Felicia returns to Kembleford from Africa, having split from her lover Benedict Northam. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Maisie Raine
programme.desc: Maisie's errant brother Kelvin asks her to investigate the theft of money he won at a casino, but as inquiries proceed, the main suspect is assaulted, giving the case a sinister twist. Police drama, starring Pauline Quirke, Paul Reynolds and Eve Matheson

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The body of a missing computer expert turns up in a hospital morgue under a false name, so the team reopens the investigation into his disappearance. The dead man worked for the Metropolitan Police, giving him access to a great deal of sensitive information - added to which, he was in contact with a group of online hackers. But he died of natural causes - so why was his body hidden, and by who? As the seasoned detectives unravel the mystery, Brian struggles to accept Steve as the new Ucos member, forcing Esther to intervene. Guest starring Sarah Smart and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A diplomat is killed while attending the latest round of Middle East talks, and MI5 suspect a link with a shadowy group known as the November Committee - but become involved in a race against time when it appears a pro-Palestinian British MP is next on the assassins' hit list. The case is complicated further when a link is discovered between the organisation and Harry's daughter. Guest starring Anton Lesser and Corin Redgrave

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A car bomb explodes outside a woman's home before her son suffocates in a sealed lab at the university where he was working on an anti-ageing cream. The detectives learn the family had been targeted by animal rights activists and discover £20,000 was paid into the chemist's account by a pharmaceutical company shortly before his death

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Hospital-grade morphine appears on the streets of Sun Hill and is soon traced back to St Hugh's, where the doctor in charge of the internal investigation proves less than cooperative with the police. Under pressure from Will, Dan admits his debt to illegal steroid use and agrees to throw his big fight, but gets carried away with the atmosphere and knocks his opponent out. Tony pays his last respects as his father passes away peacefully

programme.desc: The presenters explore a bungalow near the coast in Dymchurch in Kent, a detached house in Glanamman in Carmarthenshire and a terraced house in Thornton Heath in Surrey

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in Derby hit the streets to arrest dealers making money on their patch, and money raised from selling criminals goods is used to help feed people at a pop-up restaurant

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The specialists investigate a serial insurance fraudster who tried to cash in on horrific terror attacks, and a GP surgery manager who blew NHS money at the bookmakers

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Caroline Hawley guide two teams scouring Spitalfields Market in the heart of London's East End for bargains to take to Catherine Southon's auction house. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out how the area around Spitalfields became known as Weaver's Town with TV historian Dan Cruickshank

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Kamini wants to escape from Trefelin but realises she is the only one who can help local tearaway Dan when he gets into trouble. Prem learns more about the miners' deteriorating health as Megan helps him translate the previous doctor's diary. Comedy drama set in the 1960s, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey by exploring the history behind some of Britain's famous pies, a dish the duo have written a book about. They also find out 'who ate all the pies' on the football pitch

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a retired couple escape to the tranquillity of Norfolk after 30 years in a busy Essex town. Now that their children have flown the nest, the pair are hoping to find a country home that's full of character for their £350,000 budget. Elsewhere, Margherita discovers Norfolk's answer to the Great Barrier Reef

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden in Bournemouth for young married couple Thomas and Rhiann, who long for a family-friendly garden with a koi carp pond

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three more treasured family heirlooms, working on a Victorian railway lamp, a musical jewellery box and a Brownie toadstool

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: A party political broadcast by the Liberal Democrats

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers Dave and Harry Rich conjure up a magical fairy theme for Tom and Heather's sloping garden in Alfreton, Derbyshire, but it remains to be seen whose design will leave the couple spellbound

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Stephen Kember is given a long-mute music box that once belonged to a professional stage magician and toy experts Julia Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch get their hands on a much-loved push-along horse that has seen better days. Other items up for repair are a water-damaged penny arcade game and an old leather-backed chair

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the third episode, crew members in the capital respond to reports of an armed male and two stabbings. Elsewhere, Adam and Orani are dispatched to a long-term heroin user whose concerned mum has called for help

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: Romantic comedy anthology series. After struggling to make romantic connections in her younger years, Darby finally falls in love. Starring Anna Kendrick

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: A year later, Darby's life and career feel stagnant. When sparks fly with Bradley, her former boss who is recently divorced and highly successful, Darby feels pressure to revisit her half-baked dream of becoming a photographer - but soon gets swept up into girlfriend duties instead. Romantic comedy anthology series, starring Anna Kendrick

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Behind the doors of the Shankly Hotel in Liverpool, which has themed party suites so guests can celebrate important moments in their lives. But with the hotel's online rating having slipped, a new general manager has been called in to shake things up, although his changes do not go down well with the staff. Elsewhere, wedding planner Ciara helps brides-to-be Trudie and Danielle, while guest relations officer Liam looks after Laura, who has checked in to celebrate her recent divorce

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Merseyside police officers attempt a dangerous rescue when a car ends up in the River Mersey with the driver trapped inside. Police officers are shocked after an assault in Poole

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Martell Maxwell explore a detached house in Strood in Kent, a prefab bungalow in Alvaston, Derby and a large semi-detached in Caterham, Surrey

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: How a violent drug dealer lost his cash and shoe collection when the Metropolitan Police tracked him down. Plus, a rare Mercedes goes up for sale at the proceeds of crime auction

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The investigators look into an IT support worker shamelessly helping himself to hospital computers and a gang illegally fly-tipping on an industrial scale

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and David Harper help two teams in Hungerford, Berkshire, while Eric Knowles visits Sandham Memorial Chapel to find out about local war artist Stanley Spencer

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: An unusual van arrives in the village as Prem devises a cunning plan to diagnose the miners' health problems, but a furious Richard Sharpe is determined to turn the locals against the doctor. Meanwhile, Gina receives shocking news, and Dr Elwyn's crucial diary goes missing. Drama, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the popularity of takeaway food in Britain and discuss the various ways that it has evolved over the years. The culinary duo also rustle up some of their favourite dishes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Devon on a house-hunting mission with a professional singer, who has a £670,000 budget to buy a country home. Nicki also heads to Dartmoor National Park

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden with a dated patio into a modern, tranquil outdoor space for Amy and Kiran in Slough

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a kids' drum kit from the 1920s that has been beaten into submission, a vintage oil lamp and a 100-year-old fret saw. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: An altercation between Isaac and Jack threatens to turn ugly, leaving Amy shocked by her dad's behaviour. Callum is set up with a support officer after the fatal car accident. Ian takes drastic action to pay back Max, Mick's suspicions of Frankie continue, and Jags questions his role as a rent collector - angering both Habiba and Suki

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Checking into the Shankly this week is a party of 41 stags, who are being looked after by trainee guest relations manager James. But the morning before the lads arrive, he is nowhere to be found. Owner Lawrence challenges staff to set up a glamping festival on the roof in just one week, a loved-up couple need a last-minute bridal suite and a bride-to-be brings her friends to the hotel for her second hen party this year

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A light-hearted look back at 15 years of the business challenge, beginning with some of the most unforgettable candidates. These are the wheeler-dealers, the walking egos, the sharp-suited entrepreneurs and the jokers who make the show what it is - from the sales patter of Paul back in series one and tenacious Ruth Badger 12 months later, right up to the barrow-boy banter of Thomas last year. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the political debate, inviting a panel of guests from the worlds of politics, media and showbusiness to answer questions on subjects that have made the headlines over the past seven days

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Fergus Walsh are joined by BBC correspondents and commentators for their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing champion Katherine Grainger, former Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson and decorated 400m runner Christine Ohuruogu join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Documentary using bodycam and CCTV footage to tell the stories of the emergency service workers assaulted in the line of duty

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Today's auction property-developing projects take an incredible 14 days to more than two and a half years to complete. Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Lucy Alexander visit the properties in Sandgate in Kent and Bolton in Lancashire as well as a plot of land in Enfield in north London at the start of the projects, following the progress until they're finished

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A fraudster has his expensive Range Rover taken off him to be sold at auction and police step in when they suspect an elderly man's home has been taken over by drug dealers

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: A scam artist faking disability to claim undeserved payments. Plus, the activities of a prolific dental fraudster. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross looks back at some of the most eclectic sporting items bought by the teams, and offers advice on how to spot a bargain. The experts also take part in a special Bargain Hunt Games

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The mining village of Trefelin prepares for the annual summer fete, while Kamini goes in search of Dan, only to discover more than she bargained for. Megan pleads with Prem to stay and help the miners fight for justice, leading to a confrontation with Sharpe. Drama, starring Ayesha Dharker and Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the popularity of sugar-rich foods in Britain, and try to make their own versions of their favourite childhood sweets. The duo also prepare a cheesecake and a sweet-and-sour dish

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson visits Suffolk on a grand property search with a couple hot-footing it from the other side of the world to set down roots in the British countryside for their £900,000 budget. While in the county, Jules meets a dynamic dairy farming family whose customers are queuing up to buy their fresh milk straight from the farm's vending machine

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Alison and Janice love Australia and want a relaxing, Oz-inspired 'bolt hole', complete with cracked earth-beach look, water feature, palm trees and fire bowl in their Goring-by-Sea home, so the Rich Brothers and Charlie Dimmock head to West Sussex to see how they can help

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: An unusual piece of sporting history arrives for the attention of silversmith Brenton West and leather expert Suzie Fletcher - a decorative belt awarded to a Victorian speed walker. A 1950s metal mobo roundabout and a dented trumpet are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling legend Chris Hoy, Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard and Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by radio presenter Sara Cox, comedian Jason Manford, journalist Nick Robinson and actor Harry Shearer, who aim to help the team captains deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda, Max and Sharon team up for Frankie's Drag Bingo at the Prince Albert, while Mick works behind the bar - worried about his wife being around so much booze. Mitch goes to see Gray but he doesn't get the response he was expecting

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and producer Damian Lewis hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 60th series. Journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events. This new series comes from Hammersmith's Riverside Studios, following the previous run's virtual set-up where the panellists filmed from each of their respective homes throughout lockdown, and is planned to also see the re-introduction of a studio audience

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy goes to extreme lengths to impress her psychologist boyfriend, much to Agnes's disgust, while Rory locates the perfect apartment for him and Dino - the problem is, it's in Kilbride, meaning he'll have to learn to drive. Mark prepares to emigrate to Australia. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as the foul-mouthed mother-of-six

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host returns with a stellar line-up of guests in a show that sees him joined by esteemed actor Rupert Everett, comedian and actress Lolly Adefope and stand-up comedian and writer Sara Pascoe in the studio, and music legend Dolly Parton and Emmy-winning British star Riz Ahmed remotely. Plus, Roisin Murphy performs her single Murphy's Law and members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Awkwafina stars as Nora Lin in this scripted comedy series based on her real-life beginnings in Queens, New York. Raised alongside her cousin by her father and grandmother, Nora leans on her family as she navigates young adulthood. In this opening episode, she takes her first steps towards financial independence in an attempt to move out of her childhood home

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora and Grandma go to Atlantic City, where Nora gambles and runs into an old acquaintance on the boardwalk. Meanwhile, Grandma and her friends battle to use a casino power outlet. Comedy, starring Awkwafina and Lori Tan Chinn

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A light-hearted look back at 15 years of the business challenge, beginning with some of the most unforgettable candidates. These are the wheeler-dealers, the walking egos, the sharp-suited entrepreneurs and the jokers who make the show what it is - from the sales patter of Paul back in series one and tenacious Ruth Badger 12 months later, right up to the barrow-boy banter of Thomas last year. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team takes on the challenge of restoring an 18th-century French clock by a renowned maker, a 1960s radiogram, and a Victorian botanical microscope

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a practical but fun garden for a St Albans family, with play structures for the children and a place to grow fruit and vegetables

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The farmers settle their livestock into winter housing as the first frosts come in. In the Lake District, vet Dani prepares to perform pregnancy tests on all her cows - while six months pregnant herself. The outcome of the tests will determine the future income of the farm. High on the uplands of Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan prepare to undertake a new venture, and in Aberdeenshire, Isla and George fight to save a dangerously dehydrated deer calf

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin heads to Exeter to shows former DJ Tricia O'Neall and teacher Phil Briggs the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: The septic tank in the Turners' rickety house explodes, and to make matters worse, Shay and Arlo are having difficulties at school. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman sets the remaining contestants three challenges designed to test different fabric handling skills on children's clothing. Firstly, they must carefully follow a pattern to make baby grows from stretch cotton jersey and then show they can handle slippery satin and chiffon by transforming an adult bridesmaid's dress into a garment for a boy or a girl. Finally, they have to create perfectly fitted woollen capes, before judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young decide who leaves the competition

programme.title: Cities: Nature's New Wild
programme.desc: Animals displaced from their habitats by human expansion, including elephants in Sri Lanka and capuchin monkeys on the Pacific coast of Costa Rica. The programme also examines situations where humans have learned to live alongside their animal neighbours, including a heron that has been visiting an Amsterdam house twice a day for 17 years and a project to preserve manatees in Florida that has saved them from being an endangered species

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Natasha Raskin search for antiques at Watts Gallery in Surrey, and Paul Martin uncovers the story of the ceramicist and designer Mary Watts

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Veterinary surgeon Robin goes head to head with experienced breeder, 81 year-old Di, with their diminutive Dexter cattle in the National Competition at the Royal Norfolk Show

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on wartime Hull and the consequences of a specific bomb that fell on the city's 6th Avenue, flattening two houses and changing the lives of two families for ever. The Taylors lost baby Peter and 14-year-old Vera was very badly burned. Next door, Mrs Owens had put three of her children under the stairs for safety, but they didn't survive. Contributors include Mrs Owens' daughter Janet, who was born after the war and has many questions about what happened to her siblings. Here she meets the surviving Taylor sisters to talk about the event

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary explores how families use food to create their own sense of home, including a father and daughter who have taken their home cooking to the streets with their own food van. Later in the programme, Mary makes some of her own family favourites, including a hearty bolognaise bake with a burrata and heritage tomato salad, and a paneer and roast vegetable curry

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the fourth episode, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite chocolate recipes, including her 'Money Can't Buy You Happiness' brownies, a chocolate flan, and snack sized madeleines

programme.title: The Shipman Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The final edition opens by looking at the first report of the public inquiry into the deaths of Harold Shipman's patients, concluding that while working as a GP he had murdered 260 of them. Chris Wilson explores the reaction to the staggering total, exploring how attention began to focus on the killer as experts and commentators came up with a range of psychological profiles and theories to try to understand why one, seemingly ordinary doctor had killed so many people

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Leading performers on the stand-up circuit share their light-hearted views on modern life in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. Tonight, the host Henning Wehn welcomes Lucy Porter and Guz Khan to the stage

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Action from the final match of the five-game series, held at Incora County Ground in Derby, as both sides looked for a morale-boosting victory ahead of the ICC T20 World Cup

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: When Earn receives a cheque, he's determined to treat his girlfriend, Van, to a fancy night out. Meanwhile, Paper Boi and Darius head into the recording studio with Clark County, who is considered one of the hottest rappers in town, to see what new 'hot tracks' they can collaborate on

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn discovers just how weird white people can be when he accompanies Van to Helen, Georgia, to attend the traditional Germanic festival celebration known as Fasnacht that she frequented as a child

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Al's trip to get a haircut turns into a whistle-stop tour round Atlanta in his barber's car. Yet all he really wanted was to have a trim. Comedy drama, starring Brian Tyree Henry

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: At Swanage lifeboat station, brothers Matt and Gavin, following in their father's footsteps, race to rescue a pair of novice paddleboarders before they are swept out into the busy shipping lanes. On the north east coast, the Withernsea crew scours the North Sea for a local fisherman and friend who is missing at sea, while in Blackpool, a volunteer's wedding day is interrupted by an urgent call for help

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges a team of doctors to diagnose a young woman struggling with dramatic weight gain and hair loss, and another who has suddenly lost her sight in one eye. In the last few years, 26-year-old Hayley has gained 20kg and gone from a size 4-6 to a size 18-20, without changing her diet. She also struggles with muscle weakness and constant fatigue, and her hair has started falling out. While the experts begin their investigations into Hayley's complex case, Michael introduces them to another young woman who is struggling with frightening symptoms. In 2017, while on a camping holiday with her mum in the Lake District, Lotte developed a severe headache that has never stopped since, and she is now unable to see anything out of her left eye

programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three more treasured family heirlooms, working on a Victorian railway lamp, a musical jewellery box and a Brownie toadstool

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden in Bournemouth for young married couple Thomas and Rhiann, who long for a family-friendly garden with a koi carp pond

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce shares some of the best unseen items from recent Roadshows around the UK - from rare space memorabilia to a jewel-encrusted dagger, and a well-loved rocking horse that has entertained three generations of one family

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Quiz show in which a team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Antiques-buying challenge presented by Paul Martin, in which a photographer and an upcycler are shown the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles in Stafford

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Gossip about what happened to George's late wife has taken over the town, and he soon comes to realise that if he wants a quiet life, he will have to accept it

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman welcomes the eight remaining contestants back to the sewing room to take on three demanding challenges related to lingerie. First they follow a pattern for a bra, which proves testing for even the most dexterous of sewers, before transforming charity shop silk scarves into pieces of lingerie and finish off by making luxury robes for their male and female models. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young then decide who leaves the competition

programme.title: Elephant Family & Me
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which Gordon Buchanan encounters a family of African elephants in the wilds of Kenya, and attempts to gain their trust so he can observe them up close in their natural habitat. He enlists the aid of an expert from the David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust to learn about their behaviour as he follows an elephant with a new calf, and discovers what it takes for the youngest of the world's largest land animals to survive in the face of a challenging environment

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the Royal Marines Museum at Southsea in Hampshire, antiques expert Charles Hanson uncovers a navigational tool and Catherine Southon enthuses about a decorative Japanese cabinet dating back to the 1870s. Plus, Paul Martin finds out what it was like to serve as Royal Marine commando

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Shrewsbury Food Festival plays host to first-generation farmer Sam, with his rare breed of pork, and the family run Kerry Vale Vineyard, selling their English wine

programme.title: Live Women's FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Manchester City v Arsenal (Kick-off 7.15pm). All the action from the concluding semi-final, held at the Academy Stadium. City lifted the trophy for the second time in three seasons in 2019 and defeated Leicester City 2-1 in the quarter-finals, but will have to be at their best against the Gunners, who overcame rivals Tottenham Hotspur 4-0 in the last eight

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Julia finds that working from home is impossible due to all the tasks she has to do for her family. Meg tells her this is her 'Mother's Load' - all the things that no-one else will do, but need to be done to keep the home running. Julia removes herself to the cafe to work, but even there finds it's not that easy to get motivated. A possible solution presents itself when she shares a table with a fellow freelancer, but is Julia taking advantage of him? Meanwhile, much to her surprise, Liz finds herself bored to tears when her youngest starts nursery and she is alone during the day, and Kevin has problems when he takes on responsibility for a school fund-raising project

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian dissects the week's news using stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction. Joining him in an attempt to make sense of the world around them are Miles Jupp, Jamali Maddix and Kiri Pritchard-McLean

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: An overweight man turns up at a boxing gym and asks to be turned into a killing machine, much to the exasperation of the trainer. Short buddy comedy, written by and starring Nick Helm, with Lloyd Everitt and Jonathan Pointing

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for chub on the river Wye in Herefordshire, Bob making an unexpected arrival by rowing boat. They are joined on today's trip by Dr Anand Patel from the Centre for Men's Health for a discussion of medical matters, and spend the night in a converted dovecote in the idyllic grounds of a 700-year-old manor house

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: Harry Hill focuses on the history of home improvement shows. From Do It Yourself with Barry Bucknell to Grand Designs, Harry reveals the tricks of the trade when making these shows

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Darius answers an advertisement to pick up a free piano and meets the creepy owner, who is more interested in striking up a conversation. Donald Glover and Lakeith Stanfield star

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Van and her friends score exclusive invitations to rapper Drake's New Year's Eve party and have big hopes of meeting the host. However, things do not turn out as planned. Comedy drama, starring Zazie Beetz

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: After getting into an argument with his girlfriend, Paper Boi storms off, gets mugged, gets lost in the woods, and meets a creepy old man who will not stop following and taunting him. Comedy drama, starring Brian Tyree Henry

programme.title: The Romantics and Us with Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian explores the enduring and powerful legacy the Romantics have left on the modern world. In this episode, Simon explores how Romantic artists created the secular icons of the modern democratic age, whose power continues to inspire the passions of revolt to this day. He considers the elixir of rebellion and the idea - so powerful in the words and images of William Blake - that imaginative passion can conquer mechanical logic and can create an art for the people. With contributions from actors Harriet Walter and Christopher Eccleston, hip-hop artist Testament and the French street artist PBoy

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste learn about controlling emotions, discovering how adrenaline-packed activities can be a root to inner peace

programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a kids' drum kit from the 1920s that has been beaten into submission, a vintage oil lamp and a 100-year-old fret saw. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden with a dated patio into a modern, tranquil outdoor space for Amy and Kiran in Slough

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea repairs a memorial to First World War soldiers from the village of Norton, and a woman seeks help restoring a teddy bear that belonged to her late stepfather. Plus, Geoff Harvey gets to work on a German-built fruit machine and silversmith Brenton West takes on the repair of a precious solid silver trophy presented by the Royal Navy

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest matches, including Wigan Warriors v St Helens, Salford Red Devils v Warrington Wolves, and Huddersfield Giants v Hull Kingston Rovers

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ricky Wilson presents an appeal on behalf of the Teenage Cancer Trust, a national charity offering care and support services to young people living with cancer

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Nottingham as Carol Gardner and Jane Anderson use their own cash to buy and sell antiques to try to make the most profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George is getting a lot of unexpected attention from the local women and things come to a head at Fiona's museum fundraiser. Arlo has a run-in at school with the McNamara family

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Patrick Grant and Esme Young set the seven remaining contestants the challenge of tackling techniques and garment styles from other cultures and continents. They begin with a hard-to-follow pattern for a Chinese qipao-style top that has an asymmetrical neckline, fiddly zip insertion and is made with a fabric that frays, then they are given the task of transforming saris into items of clothing with a distinctly Eastern flavour and by creating West Africa-inspired dresses. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: Elephant Family & Me
programme.desc: The second of two programmes in which Gordon Buchanan encounters a family of African elephants in the wilderness of Kenya, and attempts to gain their trust so he can observe them up close in their natural habitat

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents a special edition, revisiting some of the historic venues from the series' history. Paul also finds out about the scandalous Georgians of Lydiard Park

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three passionate young farmers - the next generation of rising talent - battle it out at the Farmers Weekly Awards, hoping to be crowned Young Farmer of the Year

programme.title: Simply Nigella
programme.desc: A pared-down approach to cuisine in which the domestic goddess reveals that what people cook and how they prepare it really can make them feel better and more alive. Nigella prepares her avocado toast with radishes for breakfast, a Thai noodle dish with cinnamon and prawns, and a laid-back family supper of seeded lamb ribs, served with a feta and avocado salad. Nigella also serves her warm-spiced cauliflowers and 'chicksalad' and a sweet treat in the form of an apricot almond cake

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The presenter enters the glitzy world of awards and prizes - for books, movies, as well as poetry, museums and gardens - examining how they influence what art is created and what culture society consumes. She also reflects on if the pandemic has helped people see beyond the glamour and red carpets and reassess what prizes are really for. Contributors include Emmy-nominated actor Brian Cox, and Bernardine Evaristo, who last year shared the Booker Prize with Margaret Atwood

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: A look at the work of mountain vets at the ancient Kingdom of Mourne, Northern Ireland. Cahir is racing to an urgent call-out from a local pedigree sheep breeder, whose prize ewe is having difficulty in labour, and has to perform an emergency C-section. Nuala diagnoses a deep-rooted infection, finding her only choice is to put the calf down or take on a challenging amputation. Meanwhile, Jennie sees a newborn lamb brought to the clinic by its dedicated young owner, farmer Stephen

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Jennie drives into the mountains to help with a case that reminds her that being a Mountain Vet is anything but glamorous, as she has to lance a giant boil the size of a watermelon on the leg of a cow. Julie, Castle Vets' head nurse, has seen most things in her long career, but today is a first for her, as a rare wild animal has been brought in, found lying at the side of the road and before assessing what's wrong with it, she must first find out what it is

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don revels in the early autumn colour in his garden, reviews how the tomatoes he grew from a late sowing have got on this season and gives advice on harvesting and storing apples. Rachel de Thame reveals how the new garden project she started in the spring has developed over the summer and Fish, of the rock band Marillion, gives a tour of his garden and reveals how it has helped his mental health as well as the creative process of making music. Plus, in Germany, Arit Anderson finds out about the transformation of a disused industrial site into a thriving and beautiful park

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools welcomes guest Robert Plant, inviting the Led Zeppelin frontman to talk him through his musical journey, loves and influences, illustrated by classic clips from the show's archive. Plus, Declan McKenna joins Jools in the studio in a special performance at the piano of Be an Astronaut, from his recently released and critically acclaimed second album Zeros, a record heavily influenced by his '70s glam heroes. There's also music from London Grammar

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Baltar is dumbfounded when Number Six shows up in Galactica's CIC claiming to be a woman called Shelley Godfrey. He is even more distressed when she accuses him of being a traitor against humanity, Sci-fi drama, starring Edward James Olmos, James Callis and Tricia Helfer

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Kara feels guilty when she is ordered to torture a Cylon prisoner to find out where a bomb has been hidden on the Galactica. The crew members hear a nuclear warhead is poised to explode on board, but it takes time for them to believe the rumour is true

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Paper Boi is quick to regret his decision to play a show at a college campus that Earn arranged. Comedy drama about a rapper and his manager

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: A flashback to Earn and Al's adolescence, where the authenticity of Earn's shirt is embarrassingly called into question at school

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn runs around town trying to finish everything before catching a plane so that his rapper cousin Alfred, aka Paper Boi, can go on a European tour with Clark County. Comedy drama, starring Donald Glover, about an aspiring music manager trying to turn his life around by making his rapper cousin a star

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The programme hears from whistleblowers working inside the Government's new coronavirus tracking system. They are so concerned about NHS Test and Trace that they are speaking out to reveal chaos, technical problems, confusion, wasted resources and a system that does not appear to them to be working. There are also interviews with members of local public health teams, who say they have largely been ignored by the Government in favour of the private companies hired to run the new centralised tracking system

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker revisits documentaries exploring the commodification of forbidden desires, taking in subjects ranging from the porn industry to the US opioid crisis. He is reunited with porn star JJ, who has turned his back on his old career to begin a new life in eastern Europe, and drug addict Katilia, who is still struggling to turn her life around

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Live Women's FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Birmingham City v Everton (Kick-off 7.15pm). All the action from the opening semi-final at Damson Park, as the sides battle it out for a place in the Wembley final on October 31. Birmingham defeated Brighton & Hove Albion on penalties in the quarter-finals, while Everton won 2-1 against Chelsea

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Parkrun started out in London as a simple idea to get friends together for a time trial and a coffee, but now attracts more than 235,000 participants around the globe every weekend. In this film, double Olympic gold medallist Kelly Holmes explores the popularity of the events and the mental health benefits of running in the build-up to a new Parkrun in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: The king continues to publicly dispute the legitimacy of the high court but after two days of witness testimonies, the evidence against him is overwhelming. When Charles enters the courtroom on the third day, he is prepared to compromise but it is too late - he is found guilty of treason and is sentenced to be executed

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: In a small workshop in a country house in Wiltshire, silversmith Simon Benney makes distinctive jewellery and household objects for the royal family and private clients. Simon is following in the footsteps of his father, Gerald, who revolutionised the design of British silverware in the post-war era. This film follows the making of an engraved, gold and diamond pendant by Simon, featuring a trademark enamel finish using techniques his father learnt from Faberge

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of the craftspeople behind the manufacture, maintenance and restoration of Steinway pianos, from the German factory workers bending the frames and polishing the veneers to longstanding British restorer Jeff about to retire from the company. Steinway supplies pianos to the royal household as well as many leading performers, and the film goes behind the scenes as renowned pianist Lang Lang prepares for a concert at the Royal Albert Hall

programme.title: The Secret History of Writing
programme.desc: Tracing the history of the written word. The second episode examines how the changing technology of writing has shaped the course of world history

programme.title: Black Classical Music: The Forgotten History
The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: Timothy Spall: Somewhere at Sea
programme.desc: The third leg of Tim and Shane's journey takes them from the Cumbrian port of Whitehaven to Portpatrick near Stranraer. Their route includes stops on the Isle of Man, where they get to see their son Rafe filming BBC Two drama The Shadow Line, and in Belfast, home to Tim's favourite pub

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a southwestern scene clear blue skies, dry mountains and graceful river

programme.title: North By Northwest (1959)
programme.desc: A suave advertising executive is mistaken for an American intelligence agent by a foreign spy and finds himself drawn into a world of espionage and adventure, leading to a high-tension cross-country chase made more perilous when the FBI intervenes. Alfred Hitchcock's thriller, starring Cary Grant, James Mason, Eva Marie Saint, Leo G Carroll and Martin Landau

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Exploring the relationship between director Alfred Hitchcock and his stars, featuring interviews with the man himself, Kim Novak, Tippi Hedren, Joan Fontaine, Janet Leigh and Sean Connery. Sylvia Syms narrates

programme.title: Churchill and the Movie Mogul
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the friendship and collaboration between Winston Churchill and the film producer Alexander Korda. In 1934, Korda signed Churchill up as a screenwriter and historical advisor, and Churchill provided script notes for Korda's productions and penned a screenplay. When the Second World War broke out, Korda was sent on a mission to Hollywood to help bring America into the war, with positive results

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: How Australian cinema celebrates the endurance of outsiders, whether they are newcomers to a strange new land in They're a Weird Mob and Wake in Fright, or locals out of step with the mainstream in Evil Angels, Muriel's Wedding and Priscilla, Queen of the Desert

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The British director reveals the films that have influenced her life and career, including One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest, The Talented Mr Ripley, Brokeback Mountain and Claire Denis's French masterpiece Beau Travail. Sam also provides insight into the making of her most recent movie, A Million Little Pieces, and reflects on her early introduction to cinema, as well as her experience of moving to Hollywood to live and work

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a southwestern scene clear blue skies, dry mountains and graceful river

programme.title: Timothy Spall: Somewhere at Sea
programme.desc: The third leg of Tim and Shane's journey takes them from the Cumbrian port of Whitehaven to Portpatrick near Stranraer. Their route includes stops on the Isle of Man, where they get to see their son Rafe filming BBC Two drama The Shadow Line, and in Belfast, home to Tim's favourite pub

programme.title: Churchill and the Movie Mogul
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the friendship and collaboration between Winston Churchill and the film producer Alexander Korda. In 1934, Korda signed Churchill up as a screenwriter and historical advisor, and Churchill provided script notes for Korda's productions and penned a screenplay. When the Second World War broke out, Korda was sent on a mission to Hollywood to help bring America into the war, with positive results

programme.title: Timothy Spall: Somewhere at Sea
programme.desc: Tim and Shane navigate their way through ports and islands on the west coast of Scotland, but the misty weather affords them little time for sight-seeing. They then sail the Princess Matilda along the Caledonian Canal to the east coast, where they search for a place to moor their barge for the winter

programme.title: Sounds of the Eighties
programme.desc: Showcasing a selection of musical performances from the BBC archives, featuring Echo and the Bunnymen, Michael Stipe, the Smiths, the Jesus and Mary Chain, the Cure, Lone Justice and the Pixies

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Musical recap of a new decade and a new era as hip-hop, dance and indie flavours began to find a place in the mainstream charts. Top of the Pops continued to cater the best performances every Thursday to the British public with lots of dry ice, handheld cameras and a small but noisy studio audience. This compilation celebrates with some of the best studio performances of the biggest hits including break-out appearances by Adamski featuring Seal, Vanilla Ice, Beats International, EMF, the KLF Primal Scream and Snap! Plus, plenty of girl power from Betty Boo, Sinead O'Connor, Maria McKee, En Vogue, Kylie Minogue and Tina Turner

programme.title: Top of the Pops: The Story of 1990
programme.desc: A look back at the musical tastes of the early 1990s, when the new pop grammars of hip-hop and dance bewildered the old school institution that was the BBC's weekly chart show. Adamski, Orbital, 808 State and Snap! struggled to translate their brand of cool beats to the BBC's need for musicianship and random dancers, while the likes of Betty Boo, MC Tunes and Beats International brought the British take on hip-hop to the studio. In a year in that even saw footballer John Barnes embrace rap, these breakthrough hip-hop artists had to share the limelight with big balladeers including Sinead O'Connor and Maria McKee

programme.title: Geri's 1990s: My Drive to Freedom
programme.desc: Geri Horner follows in the footsteps of Tom Jones, Keith Richards, Boy George and Jazzie B by revisiting the decade that has defined her life and career to date. For Geri, that decade is the 1990s, when she shot to international stardom as Ginger Spice. At the height of her Spice Girls fame, Geri helped to ensure 'Girl Power' dominated the 'Cool Britannia' scene with her iconic Union Jack dress, and here she reflects on how a working-class girl from Watford came to symbolise the gender politics of a turbulent era. She also discusses the key political and social moments of the '90s, including Nelson Mandela's release, the birth of New Labour, the launch of the World Wide Web, and the death of Diana, Princess of Wales. Includes contributions by Geri's friends and family, and other media personalities from the decade

programme.title: Toots and the Maytals: Reggae Got Soul
programme.desc: A profile of Toots Hibbert, featuring an interview with the reggae musician and archive footage of his performances. The film traces his career from his early days as a singer in a Jamaican church to becoming a Grammy award-winning artist, and features contributions by Eric Clapton, Keith Richards, Jimmy Cliff, Bonnie Raitt, Willie Nelson, Marcia Griffiths and Paolo Nutini

programme.title: Toots and the Maytals at Glastonbury 2010
programme.desc: The ska group's performance on the West Holts Stage, featuring songs such as Pressure Drop, Louie Louie, Monkey Man and 54-46 Was My Number. Introduced by Mark Radcliffe

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure and Kim Appleby explore the sounds that came from Scotland, Ireland and Wales. They start in Glasgow with the American influences that shaped a substantial part of Scottish music, look at the punk and folk backdrop to Irish music and, finally, delve into the Welsh merger of folk and punk. The programme features evocative archive, music and interviews with significant figures of the era including Bob Geldof, Clare Grogan from Altered Images, Pat Kane from Hue and Cry, Moya Brennan of Clannad and Mike Peters from legendary Welsh band the Alarm

programme.title: Top of the Pops: The Story of 1990
programme.desc: A look back at the musical tastes of the early 1990s, when the new pop grammars of hip-hop and dance bewildered the old school institution that was the BBC's weekly chart show. Adamski, Orbital, 808 State and Snap! struggled to translate their brand of cool beats to the BBC's need for musicianship and random dancers, while the likes of Betty Boo, MC Tunes and Beats International brought the British take on hip-hop to the studio. In a year in that even saw footballer John Barnes embrace rap, these breakthrough hip-hop artists had to share the limelight with big balladeers including Sinead O'Connor and Maria McKee

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson

Having been chosen to join a top academy on the mainland he's preparing to move to Glasgow permanently - but not before playing in the most important annual match on the island.

It’s fish flavour for the penguins, lettuce lollies for Marion the tortoise and a huge blood lolly for the big cats. programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: Romantic comedy anthology series. After struggling to make romantic connections in her younger years, Darby finally falls in love. Starring Anna Kendrick

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: A year later, Darby's life and career feel stagnant. When sparks fly with Bradley, her former boss who is recently divorced and highly successful, Darby feels pressure to revisit her half-baked dream of becoming a photographer - but soon gets swept up into girlfriend duties instead. Romantic comedy anthology series, starring Anna Kendrick

programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and adventurer explores the hidden pieces of history from the Second World War. Wales made a huge contribution to the war effort and the remote countryside was witness to many military activities. Remnants from that time can still be found, but they lie hidden. Will seeks some of them out by visiting plane crash sites on the Brecon Beacons, uncovering an old training academy on the Great Orme, gaining exclusive access to an MoD training base that played a vital role in the conflict and tracks down the secret bunkers of a home-based army of saboteurs

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling legend Chris Hoy, Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard and Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda, Max and Sharon team up for Frankie's Drag Bingo at the Prince Albert, while Mick works behind the bar - worried about his wife being around so much booze. Mitch goes to see Gray but he doesn't get the response he was expecting

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and producer Damian Lewis hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 60th series. Journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events. This new series comes from Hammersmith's Riverside Studios, following the previous run's virtual set-up where the panellists filmed from each of their respective homes throughout lockdown, and is planned to also see the re-introduction of a studio audience

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Rhod thinks he has a great chance of becoming a model - after all, he was voted Wales' 14th sexiest man. However, his dreams of strutting along catwalks in Milan and Paris are shattered by unimpressed fashion guru Julien Macdonald, so he has to lower his expectations somewhat, taking him into parts of the industry he never knew existed. He strips off for a room full of budding artists, meets a woman whose hands are her fortune, and signs up with an agency that focuses on 'character' rather than good looks. Can he make it or must he face the brutal truth?

Plus, Roisin Murphy performs her single Murphy's Law and members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is proud of her working-class roots, but with the gap between rich and poor widening, she wants to know how easy it would be for her to move up a class. She overhauls her leisure activities with a spot of golf and some classical music, takes voice lessons to refine her accent and supercharges her table manners with an intense etiquette lesson. However, she soon discovers that there are obstacles in the way that can make social mobility very difficult

programme.desc: Arlene Stuart investigates the shocking increase in litter around Scottish beauty spots after lockdown and what is being done to tackle it. Anne Lundon looks back to a village once branded the dirtiest in Scotland, that campaigned to clean up the mess on their doorstep. Dougie Vipond finds out how to roam responsibly in the outdoors as a dog owner and teams up with Nick Nairn to celebrate the best from the natural larder, cooking wood pigeon outdoors on the shores of the Lake of Menteith

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling legend Chris Hoy, Winter Olympic skeleton gold medallist Amy Williams, former England cricketer Matthew Hoggard and Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda, Max and Sharon team up for Frankie's Drag Bingo at the Prince Albert, while Mick works behind the bar - worried about his wife being around so much booze. Mitch goes to see Gray but he doesn't get the response he was expecting

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and producer Damian Lewis hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 60th series. Journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events. This new series comes from Hammersmith's Riverside Studios, following the previous run's virtual set-up where the panellists filmed from each of their respective homes throughout lockdown, and is planned to also see the re-introduction of a studio audience

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Cathy goes to extreme lengths to impress her psychologist boyfriend, much to Agnes's disgust, while Rory locates the perfect apartment for him and Dino - the problem is, it's in Kilbride, meaning he'll have to learn to drive. Mark prepares to emigrate to Australia. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as the foul-mouthed mother-of-six

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host returns with a stellar line-up of guests in a show that sees him joined by esteemed actor Rupert Everett, comedian and actress Lolly Adefope and stand-up comedian and writer Sara Pascoe in the studio, and music legend Dolly Parton and Emmy-winning British star Riz Ahmed remotely. Plus, Roisin Murphy performs her single Murphy's Law and members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Awkwafina stars as Nora Lin in this scripted comedy series based on her real-life beginnings in Queens, New York. Raised alongside her cousin by her father and grandmother, Nora leans on her family as she navigates young adulthood. In this opening episode, she takes her first steps towards financial independence in an attempt to move out of her childhood home
