programme.desc: After a heavy night partying, Alison and Mike wake to find their house in disarray and with the clock ticking until a potentially crucial business partner comes to view the venue, the couple attempt a mass clean up. The ghosts are all acting very strangely, and Alison struggles to piece together the events of the night before to figure out what happened to them

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor and producer Damian Lewis hosts as the satirical quiz returns for its 60th series. Journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events. This new series comes from Hammersmith's Riverside Studios, following the previous run's virtual set-up where the panellists filmed from each of their respective homes throughout lockdown, and is planned to also see the re-introduction of a studio audience

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host returns with a stellar line-up of guests in a show that sees him joined by esteemed actor Rupert Everett, comedian and actress Lolly Adefope and stand-up comedian and writer Sara Pascoe in the studio, and music legend Dolly Parton and Emmy-winning British star Riz Ahmed remotely. Plus, Roisin Murphy performs her single Murphy's Law and members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Awkwafina stars as Nora Lin in this scripted comedy series based on her real-life beginnings in Queens, New York. Raised alongside her cousin by her father and grandmother, Nora leans on her family as she navigates young adulthood. In this opening episode, she takes her first steps towards financial independence in an attempt to move out of her childhood home

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Nora and Grandma go to Atlantic City, where Nora gambles and runs into an old acquaintance on the boardwalk. Meanwhile, Grandma and her friends battle to use a casino power outlet. Comedy, starring Awkwafina and Lori Tan Chinn

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A light-hearted look back at 15 years of the business challenge, beginning with some of the most unforgettable candidates. These are the wheeler-dealers, the walking egos, the sharp-suited entrepreneurs and the jokers who make the show what it is - from the sales patter of Paul back in series one and tenacious Ruth Badger 12 months later, right up to the barrow-boy banter of Thomas last year. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The programme hears from whistleblowers working inside the Government's new coronavirus tracking system. They are so concerned about NHS Test and Trace that they are speaking out to reveal chaos, technical problems, confusion, wasted resources and a system that does not appear to them to be working. There are also interviews with members of local public health teams, who say they have largely been ignored by the Government in favour of the private companies hired to run the new centralised tracking system

programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Mike, Lisa, Darren and Gladys pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Liberal Democrats

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vinny makes a discovery, Aaron asks Bear for suggestions, and Tracy attempts to get Cain and Nate together

programme.desc: Grace tells a shocked Ed that Michael abducted Tianna, and Carla and Sarah are stunned by Nina's ideas which have been added to a client's designs. Although Ray turns down Nick's offer of an Underworld share, his donation to Oliver's medical fund leaves Tilsley amazed

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Virgin's clubhouse manager Holly showers passengers with her VIP treatment Valentine-style, while officers Terry and Dave have to deal with an unhappy upgraded passenger. Meanwhile, a full A330 could be in trouble as a new recruit has his first attempt at pushing it to the runway. Narrated by Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sarah decides to speak to Adam about getting back together, and Nina apologises to Carla for putting her own stamp on the underwear designs. programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse into the lives of those lucky enough to check into the glamorous London hotel, and those who serve them. It's the start of the new year, and after their busy festive period, the hotel has high hopes for 2020 - looking at how they can improve all areas of their service and make it the most successful year in the hotel's history. Head butler Sean is determined to make his newest recruit - 37-year-old former actor Michael - the perfect Savoy butler, and is putting him to the test. As well as having to juggle a multitude of requests, Sean wants Michael to host an exclusive cocktail party for regular VIP guests, Mr and Mrs Cuming, in their suite

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme

programme.title: In Cold Blood
programme.desc: In the 1970s, a new treatment for haemophilia known as Factor VIII was prescribed on the NHS. It infected more than 1,300 people with HIV and more than 4,000 people with Hepatitis C. As a public inquiry into the scandal reopens, this investigative feature-length documentary for ITV's Bafta-winning Exposure strand offers a window into a tragedy whose scale and impact has been relatively understated in Britain

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to New Orleans and after delving into a roadside seafood market, he cooks in Louis Armstrong Park and meets singer Robin Barnes. Lunch is courtesy of local favourite the Palace Café, where redfish is on the menu, and James finishes his stay by cooking on a balcony in the centre of the French Quarter and sipping of one of the local cocktails

programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five members of a Surrey bowling club answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Adam, Kate, Sally and Kelvin pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.desc: Vinny wonders if he should open up to Mandy, Aaron receives a letter, and Chas continues to become distant

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: The financial expert is joined by Angellica Bell for another round of advice on family finances, jobs and consumer rights, including the latest deals of the week and tips on making money go further

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jimmy the northern white-cheeked gibbon is missing the visitors and wondering when they will be back to see him again, while keepers Mick and Joe use the quiet time to build the confidence of rare Bactrian camel Genghis, helping him to venture outside of his paddock. The elephant keepers at Whipsnade are desperate for the Asian elephants to mate, although it seems there are no such problems with the small-clawed otters when keeper Laura notices they have had their first babies

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Queen Elizabeth II has mastered the art of being a monarch, both home and abroad, over her record-breaking 68-year reign. She is seen meeting world leaders such as Ronald Reagan, Mikhail Gorbachev and Donald Trump - although not all encounters go well, and the film also looks at the Queen's commitment to the Commonwealth, a personal project that has seen the old Empire transformed

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: The Blacketts intensify their campaign to secure Matthew's allegiances and Walter remains unyielding, despite the Japanese bombing Singapore. As Vera nurses Matthew through his fevered delirium, the Major is warned not to trust her. Sylvia's brother Charlie rejoins his regiment and Ehrendorf volunteers, and Matthew makes a momentous decision

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A body is found buried on the Northumberland moors which could be a missing teenager from the nearby rural community of Brindale. The girl had disappeared six weeks previously, and Vera has to break the news to her devastated family. However, the realisation that she died so close to home rather than at a music festival as originally thought creates tension among her neighbours, who are soon suspecting each other of murder. The detective delves into the vulnerable young woman's isolated existence to identify her killer

Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Black Widow, Thingamajig, Butterfly, Leopard and Flamingo perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three suitors buy an outfit for fashion entrepreneur Caz, who then goes on a date with the person whose taste in clothes they liked best

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness presents as more single men enter his 'Love Lift' in the hope of impressing his panel of 30 unattached ladies. This time, Josh, a fitness fanatic from Woking, Dan, a volcanologist from Bristol, HR manager Martin from Sunderland, and Tai, an NHS worker from Birmingham will be hoping to find romance. Plus, a chance to catch up with last week's couples

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Rib-tickling collection of camcorder calamities and home-video howlers, featuring golfing gaffes, canine calamities, animal antics, holiday howlers and amusing CCTV footage

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden hopes to create the perfect Christmas experience for Louis, who has lost his faith in the festivities, but Alan's cheapness - as well as his mother - threaten to ruin everything. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan and Herb run into Lyndsey at yoga class and Herb is tempted to break the 'bro-code,' while Walden and Laurel try to spend the night together. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer

programme.title: Love Island USA
programme.desc: American version of the hit reality show featuring a gang of gorgeous young singles gathered in one villa for the summer of their lives. Arielle Vandenburg hosts

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian enters a dog competition in which the prize is to breed with a female, while Peter and Lois try to persuade Chris that Arthur Valentine does not exist

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter's new friendship with a paramedic boosts his self-esteem, prompting him to dump his old pals, but he soon realises he cannot keep up with the man's active lifestyle

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois's parents hire a nanny to help with the children and while the Griffins initially enjoy having more time for each other, a trip away leaves their marriage in tatters

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan gets amnesia after falling off an arcade game during a CIA operation and Francine sees it as the perfect opportunity to mould him into her ideal husband. Meanwhile, Steve is drawn into the corrupt side of Roger's paper route business.

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A dark secret about the Smith family is revealed when Francine throws Stan a birthday party and invites his incarcerated father to the celebration

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the 'filming' of an episode - which is nearly ruined by a drunken Roberta, angry at having her storyline cut

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland loses his confidence and takes his family on a soul-searching journey to Africa, but gets sidetracked and spends a few days in Hawaii - where he has an epiphany

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden hopes to create the perfect Christmas experience for Louis, who has lost his faith in the festivities, but Alan's cheapness - as well as his mother - threaten to ruin everything. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan and Herb run into Lyndsey at yoga class and Herb is tempted to break the 'bro-code,' while Walden and Laurel try to spend the night together. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer

programme.title: Roswell, New Mexico
programme.desc: The town prepares for CrashCon, as Liz and Max attempt to find out who is behind a potentially deadly plan targeting the festival. Maria and Isobel take drastic measures, while Michael must do someone else's bidding. Sci-fi drama, starring Jeanine Mason and Nathan Parsons

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson guides viewers through some more weird and wonderful Guinness World Records, including the fastest time to pull an aircraft, the most watermelons punched in one minute and the longest time to sing suspended upside down

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samantha enjoys leading Sean on when he flirts with her. Deirdre and Ken meet their future son-in-law. Raquel tells Curly she has been offered a job in Malaysia

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is amazed that Tracy does not seem excited about her wedding. Robert goes missing after his stag night. Curly tells Raquel he knows she has never loved him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah and Jack receive a letter from Andy. Rachel receives some shocking news about her mother. Becky attempts to find out what is bothering Tony

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Clare finds herself under suspicion when a wild party ends in tragedy with a drugs-related death, and David is put in a tricky situation when his Aunty Peggy arrives in Aidensfield and sets up an illegal vodka still

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo try to foil a showdown at the Mucky Duck Saloon when Robin and loud Irishman Mick get into an argument. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin and Jo become concerned when Chrissy starts dating an older man - so they decide to play gooseberry. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper is astonished and thrilled when George buys her an expensive present for their anniversary. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victim's electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A crime wave hits Ashfordly train station as a number of freight wagons mysteriously vanish, giving the police a tricky case to crack, but a sandwich could provide a vital clue. Peggy has plans to make some fast money when she decides to play dirty in the local bowls tournament, while a night out in Whitby proves eventful for Walker and Helen. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor, Jonathan Kerrigan and Sophie Ward

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel feels awkward when she sees Jack again. Kim attempts to impress Alex. Chris sees Lyn, Roy, Will and Kelly stealing a key to one of the holiday village chalets

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel and Jack leave for the funeral in Sheffield. Kelly approaches Chris about a job in the office. Tony gets aggressive in the wine bar

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy fears that she might never conceive. Sean accuses Liz of spreading malicious rumours about him. Curly refuses to speak to Raquel

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly returns home to find all trace of Raquel gone, so he gets drunk and spends the evening wallowing in self-pity. Samantha is amazed to hear Sean and Liz are an item

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits his Aunt Alice, who is recovering from an operation at a nursing home, and finds himself in the middle of a mystery. A resident has been found dead and the official verdict is a heart attack, but as rumours of fraud and deception abound, Alice's friend believes someone may have conspired to commit murder

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A student is attacked on campus, and Jack takes time off from questioning two robbery suspects to catch the thug responsible before he can strike again. The trail leads him to Boxborough - where a confrontation with a belligerent solicitor provides the clue he needs to crack the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons, Charlotte Bellamy and Tazmin Malleson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are called away from a team-building exercise to investigate a fatal explosion at a haulage yard in Calham Cross. The property is owned by Territorial Army commanding officer Matt Parkes, the father of a businessman whose ruthlessness in the boardroom is matched only by his behaviour toward women. To complicate matters, the village is also home to a peace protester and a grieving mother - and Barnaby has to decide if any of these locals is capable of arson and murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with Tim Pigott-Smith, Joseph Millson, Niamh Cusack, Judy Parfitt and Nick Fletcher

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With lawyer David Hornby now a suspect, Cabbot is forced to admit the father of her baby doesn't know about her pregnancy. She then discovers band member Martin Soames' confession in the John Gaunt case file is missing and that Matt Barber's father Jack was the original investigating officer, leading Helen to believe she has the evidence she needs to push the former policeman harder. But when Banks learns of a mysterious tape recording that appears to link Jack with the Crystal Kiss, he realises he's in a race to find it if he is to have any hope of uncovering the truth. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: A Touch of Frost

The trail leads him to Boxborough - where a confrontation with a belligerent solicitor provides the clue he needs to crack the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons, Charlotte Bellamy and Tazmin Malleson

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo invite a German guest to their dinner party - but Mr Roper demonstrates that the Second World War has not ended as far as he is concerned

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin and Chrissy go babysitting, but the baby will not stop crying, and the drinks cupboard is locked. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper goes into hospital with suspected appendicitis and George looks forward to some peace and quiet - but Jerry shows up on his doorstep with other ideas. Roy Kinnear guest stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A student is attacked on campus, and Jack takes time off from questioning two robbery suspects to catch the thug responsible before he can strike again. The trail leads him to Boxborough - where a confrontation with a belligerent solicitor provides the clue he needs to crack the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons, Charlotte Bellamy and Tazmin Malleson

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl is injured in a horse riding accident, leading the police to stumble upon a sinister series of animal thefts. Helen tries to tell Rob she doesn't want a relationship with him, but worries about hurting his feelings. Meanwhile, Oscar catches Peggy and David hosting a poker tournament in the Aidensfield Arms and bars them from the pub. Rural drama starring Sophie Ward, Derek Fowlds, Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack and Rachel talk about the night before. Tara isn't happy about Alex going into business with Kim. Karen abducts Alice and drives off with her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Alice is still missing and Kathy is determined to find her. Rachel goes to see her mum's solicitor. Emma is convinced her dad hates her for all the trouble she has caused

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz visits Steve and tells him she is strapped for cash. Ken tells Deirdre he was a fool to throw their marriage away. Jack is upset that Raquel has left

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen is upset to hear Raquel has left Curly. Denise and Brian turn up saying they are taking Daniel away, so Deirdre invites Ken round for a meal

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A petty thief is impaled with a pitchfork in sleepy Midsomer Mallow, jeopardising the residents' chances of winning a 'perfect village' competition. To make matters worse, two of the judges are also killed. Guest starring Timothy West and Hannah Gordon, along with an early appearance by a young Orlando Bloom as the first victim

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A visiting American academic delivers a controversial speech, with many audience members worried his ideas could be used to target ethnic minorities. So when he is found hanged the next morning and Dr Hobson quickly discounts suicide, Lewis and Hathaway realise they have many suspects - from the local anti-racism activist who was first in voicing her disgust at the professor's beliefs to the women who had bombarded him with threatening e-mails. But then another body turns up and the case takes on a new dimension. David Soul and Patrick Baladi guest star

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the detective's old neighbour tells him she has found a gun in her teenage daughter's bedroom, his first instinct is to quietly retrieve the weapon. However, Helen persuades him to do things by the book and leads a firearms unit to take possession of it - but an innocent man is critically injured in the process, leading to an internal investigation that threatens to break up Banks's team

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin fails his catering exams and decides to apply for a job in Southampton. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The girls find it tough sharing with one man, but their problems double once Robin's friend Larry moves in. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George has already denounced the neighbours as toffee-nosed snobs, and events make Mildred wonder whether he could be right. Queenie Watts and Michael Robbins guest star, with Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An accidental shooting at Ashfordly Hall prompts Bellamy to investigate reports accusing acting estate manager Richard Bentham of exploiting youngsters for child labour, but his enquiries reveal a far more complicated situation. Peggy discovers a promising new source of revenue when she realises there is a market for guided tours of the area, but her excitement is cut short by a terrifying encounter. Rural drama, guest starring former Emmerdale actor Peter Amory, with Mark Jordon, Rupert Vansittart and Gwen Taylor

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is not happy about Sarah going off to Spain. Social services come to talk with the Cairns family. Kim attempts to seduce Lord Alex

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lord Alex tries to quietly leave Home Farm after spending the night with Kim. Jack and Rachel insist that what happened between them is over

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken and Deirdre rush to confront Denise. Jamie blames himself for Jack's accident. Alec lays into Fiona for breaking her contract. Fred has a proposal for Rita

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kelly hints to Ashley about them getting a flat. Jack is embarrassed in hospital when Martin gives him a bed bath. Ken visits a solicitor to find out his rights over Daniel

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A commercial decision to turn Midsomer Deverell's memorial garden into a tea shop proves fatal for members of the Inkpen family. DCI Barnaby's investigation unearths more than mere local hostility as he lifts the lid on long-buried secrets. Anna Calder-Marshall and Margaret Tyzack guest star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa helps out her ex-boyfriend Danny when he brings seven children from London to camp on Ruth's farm - supervised by Al and Bert - while she and Martin plan a date on the advice of Dr Timoney. However, their night at the restaurant is interrupted when Danny calls saying one of the youngsters has gone missing. Things get worse for the couple when Martin uncovers a health scare at James's first birthday party that makes all the guests leave very quickly. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is not best pleased when her new art teacher Erica wants to try out some rather alternative ideas on the children, while Dr Timoney is involved in a car accident outside the school. Penhale is confused when he is offered a transfer to Exeter, Bert decides to go back to being a handyman, and Louisa and Martin are intrigued when Dr Timoney invites them for an additional therapy session. Plus, an American tourist seeks help from the GP. Comedy drama, with a cameo appearance by Sigourney Weaver

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. On a desperate search for his abducted daughter, Banks puts pressure on Al to lead him to Jaff, without success. However, Al later offers to help - at a price - and the detective contemplates breaking every rule in the book to meet his demands. Meanwhile, the result of Cabbot's internal investigation has led to Morton's immediate suspension. With her career seemingly in the balance, Tracy in danger and Banks's relationship with Annie close to breaking point, how far will he go to keep his family together? Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

Helen tries to tell Rob she doesn't want a relationship with him, but worries about hurting his feelings. Meanwhile, Oscar catches Peggy and David hosting a poker tournament in the Aidensfield Arms and bars them from the pub. Rural drama starring Sophie Ward, Derek Fowlds, Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack and Rachel talk about the night before. Tara isn't happy about Alex going into business with Kim. Karen abducts Alice and drives off with her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Alice is still missing and Kathy is determined to find her. Rachel goes to see her mum's solicitor. Emma is convinced her dad hates her for all the trouble she has caused

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz visits Steve and tells him she is strapped for cash. Ken tells Deirdre he was a fool to throw their marriage away. Jack is upset that Raquel has left

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen is upset to hear Raquel has left Curly. Denise and Brian turn up saying they are taking Daniel away, so Deirdre invites Ken round for a meal

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A petty thief is impaled with a pitchfork in sleepy Midsomer Mallow, jeopardising the residents' chances of winning a 'perfect village' competition. To make matters worse, two of the judges are also killed. Guest starring Timothy West and Hannah Gordon, along with an early appearance by a young Orlando Bloom as the first victim

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A visiting American academic delivers a controversial speech, with many audience members worried his ideas could be used to target ethnic minorities. So when he is found hanged the next morning and Dr Hobson quickly discounts suicide, Lewis and Hathaway realise they have many suspects - from the local anti-racism activist who was first in voicing her disgust at the professor's beliefs to the women who had bombarded him with threatening e-mails. But then another body turns up and the case takes on a new dimension. David Soul and Patrick Baladi guest star

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the detective's old neighbour tells him she has found a gun in her teenage daughter's bedroom, his first instinct is to quietly retrieve the weapon. However, Helen persuades him to do things by the book and leads a firearms unit to take possession of it - but an innocent man is critically injured in the process, leading to an internal investigation that threatens to break up Banks's team

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin fails his catering exams and decides to apply for a job in Southampton. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The girls find it tough sharing with one man, but their problems double once Robin's friend Larry moves in. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George has already denounced the neighbours as toffee-nosed snobs, and events make Mildred wonder whether he could be right. Queenie Watts and Michael Robbins guest star, with Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An accidental shooting at Ashfordly Hall prompts Bellamy to investigate reports accusing acting estate manager Richard Bentham of exploiting youngsters for child labour, but his enquiries reveal a far more complicated situation. Peggy discovers a promising new source of revenue when she realises there is a market for guided tours of the area, but her excitement is cut short by a terrifying encounter. Rural drama, guest starring former Emmerdale actor Peter Amory, with Mark Jordon, Rupert Vansittart and Gwen Taylor

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is not happy about Sarah going off to Spain. Social services come to talk with the Cairns family. Kim attempts to seduce Lord Alex

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lord Alex tries to quietly leave Home Farm after spending the night with Kim. Jack and Rachel insist that what happened between them is over

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken and Deirdre rush to confront Denise. Jamie blames himself for Jack's accident. Alec lays into Fiona for breaking her contract. Fred has a proposal for Rita

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kelly hints to Ashley about them getting a flat. Jack is embarrassed in hospital when Martin gives him a bed bath. Ken visits a solicitor to find out his rights over Daniel

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A commercial decision to turn Midsomer Deverell's memorial garden into a tea shop proves fatal for members of the Inkpen family. DCI Barnaby's investigation unearths more than mere local hostility as he lifts the lid on long-buried secrets. Anna Calder-Marshall and Margaret Tyzack guest star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa helps out her ex-boyfriend Danny when he brings seven children from London to camp on Ruth's farm - supervised by Al and Bert - while she and Martin plan a date on the advice of Dr Timoney. However, their night at the restaurant is interrupted when Danny calls saying one of the youngsters has gone missing. Things get worse for the couple when Martin uncovers a health scare at James's first birthday party that makes all the guests leave very quickly. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa is not best pleased when her new art teacher Erica wants to try out some rather alternative ideas on the children, while Dr Timoney is involved in a car accident outside the school. Penhale is confused when he is offered a transfer to Exeter, Bert decides to go back to being a handyman, and Louisa and Martin are intrigued when Dr Timoney invites them for an additional therapy session. Plus, an American tourist seeks help from the GP. Comedy drama, with a cameo appearance by Sigourney Weaver

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. On a desperate search for his abducted daughter, Banks puts pressure on Al to lead him to Jaff, without success. However, Al later offers to help - at a price - and the detective contemplates breaking every rule in the book to meet his demands. Meanwhile, the result of Cabbot's internal investigation has led to Morton's immediate suspension. With her career seemingly in the balance, Tracy in danger and Banks's relationship with Annie close to breaking point, how far will he go to keep his family together? Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar saves the life of the prime minister of a British colony. Guest starring Edward Woodward

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The Baker Street sleuth is summoned to the Lake District to investigate the mysterious disappearance of an enigmatic woman. Period detective drama, guest starring Michael Jayston and Cheryl Campbell with Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Tara battle a group of rogue scientists trying to sell a deadly invention - a virus that causes people to sneeze themselves to death. Fulton Mackay (Porridge) guest stars

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the fourth day of the Grand Slam, staged at Roland Garros in Paris, where the second-round matches in the men's and women's draws are scheduled to get under way. Roger Federer and Rafael Nadal were both typically dominant at this stage last year, recording straight-sets victories over Oscar Otte and Yannick Madden respectively. In the women's event, fourth seed Kiki Bertens retired hurt against Viktoria Kuzmova, while Karolina Pliskova comfortably avoided a further upset as she prevailed 6-2, 6-2 against Kristina Kucova. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: King of the Roads
programme.desc: The Scarborough Gold Cup. Action from England's only closed road race in Scarborough. Following the Barry Sheene Classic, it was the turn of the modern machines to compete

programme.title: Hulk (2003)
programme.desc: Scientist Dr Bruce Banner turns into the rampaging green Hulk after exposure to a huge dose of gamma radiation, prompting the military to embark on a no-holds-barred mission to destroy the monster. Meanwhile, Banner's unstable father resurfaces, holding the secret to his transformation. Comic-book adventure from director Ang Lee, starring Eric Bana, Jennifer Connelly, Sam Elliott and Nick Nolte

programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Hulk (2003)
programme.desc: Scientist Dr Bruce Banner turns into the rampaging green Hulk after exposure to a huge dose of gamma radiation, prompting the military to embark on a no-holds-barred mission to destroy the monster. Meanwhile, Banner's unstable father resurfaces, holding the secret to his transformation. Comic-book adventure from director Ang Lee, starring Eric Bana, Jennifer Connelly, Sam Elliott and Nick Nolte

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur swaps the contents of his lock-up for a slot-machine full of stolen money during a trip to the fair. Unfortunately the incriminating item is passed to Morley, who uses it at a charity event, and a showdown between cops and robbers ensues. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole, Nicholas Day and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A security guard is murdered during a payroll snatch, and Regan and Carter are called in to support local investigating officers. However, bad blood between the maverick detective inspector and the force's chief superintendent going back several years manifests itself in an old allegation of corruption being rekindled. Richard Griffiths features alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman in the final episode of the crime drama

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The duo try to track down an American colonel tormented by memories his harrowing experiences while in command of an air base in Vietnam. Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter star

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon finds himself teetering between two women racing drivers - who are rivals in love as well as on the track. Adventure, guest starring Kate O'Mara

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A governess is accused of shooting dead her millionaire employer's wife on a remote bridge - but Holmes' deductive powers suggest an alternative explanation to the mystery. Period detective drama, guest starring Daniel Massey, with Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The murders of several British agents seem to be the work of an infamous spy whom Steed killed years previously. Julian Glover guest stars

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the fifth day of the Grand Slam, staged at Roland Garros in Paris, where the second-round matches in the men's and women's draws are scheduled to conclude. British number one Kyle Edmund's tournament came to an end at this stage last year when he retired hurt while two sets down against Uruguay's Pablo Cuevas, while top seed Novak Djokovic comfortably overcame lucky loser Henri Laaksonen. There were also victories for three of the leading women's stars on day five, with Serena Williams, Simona Halep and Naomi Osaka defeating Kurumi Nara, Magda Linette and Victoria Azarenka respectively. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: King of the Roads
programme.desc: More action from the Scarborough Gold Cup, where road racing favourites Dean Harrison and Lee Johnston were in action on the solo grid, and the Birchall brothers were expected to take centre-stage in the sidecar discipline

programme.title: Waterworld (1995)
programme.desc: In the future, environmental disasters have left the planet flooded. A web-footed mutant mariner helps a woman and an orphan girl search for a mythical haven of dry land, with the help of a map tattooed on the child's back - but in the process has to fend off a villainous crew of nicotine-addicted pirates. Sci-fi adventure, starring Kevin Costner, Dennis Hopper, Jeanne Tripplehorn, Tina Majorino and Rick Aviles

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Britain's most advanced computer suffers a series of acts of sabotage. Steed and Tara try to decode a cryptic message from the machine naming the culprit. Dennis Price guest stars

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the sixth day of the Grand Slam, staged at Roland Garros in Paris, where the third-round matches in the men's and women's draws are scheduled to get under way. Great Britain's Johanna Konta continued her bid for glory on day six in 2019 with a 6-2, 6-1 victory over Viktoria Kuzmova, but second seed Karolina Pliskova's tournament came to an end when she lost 6-3, 6-3 to Petra Martic. The corresponding seed in the men's event, Rafael Nadal, had no such trouble as he defeated David Goffin in four sets, and his great rival Roger Federer joined him in round four after a 6-3, 6-1, 7-6 victory over Casper Ruud. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: An Audience with Billy Connolly
programme.desc: The comedian descends on London to entertain a celebrity crowd including Twiggy, Ringo Starr, Michael Brandon, Samantha Fox and Clive James, covering topics including ageing, parties and his idea for a new national anthem

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Car Crash Global: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary featuring dash-cam video footage of a host of astonishing accidents, spectacular smashes and enthralling escapes from Scandinavia and China

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A business deal backfires badly, leaving the future of the Winchester in doubt, so Ray organises a fundraising football match against the police. However, with kick-off looming, one of Arthur's incompetent associates appears with a huge sum of money stolen from a rival businessman, leaving the the dodgy dealer seeking a way to stash the cash without it being noticed. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.desc: Harry becomes embroiled with a fascist Prince while delivering a dispatch case to an address in Rome. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.desc: Kate Quilton and Dr Javid Abdelmoneim are joined by 10 volunteers, who each have a history of failed dieting, to take part in an experiment. The goal of the project is to find out if simple lifestyle techniques can help them lose weight in just six weeks. Kate and Javid also explore why increasingly plant-based diets are being used in sports, why men find weight-loss easier when doing it as part of a team, and whether people would snack less if they realised how much exercise it would take to burn off those calories

Part-time fitness instructor and disco dancer Selvin meets Donna, who seeks a man who can make her laugh. programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris explores five countries that were part of Yugoslavia, which had an extensive state-run railway network before its break-up in the 1990s and the Balkan Wars of Independence that followed. From Slovenia, Chris heads east along an old Austro-Hungarian railway that was once used by the Orient Express, pays a visit to a very unusual railway-related battlefield in Bosnia and rides on one of the world's great mountain railways on his way to the Serbian capital Belgrade before finishing his journey in Montenegro

programme.title: Walking Britain's Roman Roads
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores Roman roads Dere Street, the northern-most Roman road in Britain running from York to Scotland, and Stanegate, a key east-west route that runs alongside the great defensive barrier of Hadrian's Wall. His journey reveals the story of Roman military and its colonial ambitions in Britain

programme.title: Lawrence of Arabia: Britain's Great Adventurer
programme.desc: The epic story of Thomas Edward Lawrence the First World War military officer who united the tribes of Arabia against the Ottoman Turkish army. The film reveals that while being one of the most enigmatic figures of the 20th century, he was also a deeply troubled man, delving into his personal torment, the secret his family were hiding, and the punishment he endured

programme.title: The Curse of King Tut
programme.desc: The 1922 discovery of Tutankhamun's tomb in Egypt's Valley of Kings remains one of the greatest archaeological finds of all time. But when Howard Carter broke through the sealed entrance to reveal thousands of priceless artefacts and the teenage body of the boy king, rumours of the Pharaoh's revenge consumed the media. And when Carter's benefactor Lord Carnarvon suddenly died, the belief that a deadly curse had been unleashed ran like wildfire. This documentary looks back at Carter and Carnarvon's discovery, and explores the truth behind the stories of the curse

programme.title: Wild Britain
programme.desc: Revealing the hidden lives of the familiar and the more unusual animals in the United Kingdom, including the golden eagle, red deer and reindeer. Cameras also follow a bold project attempting to re-tree the Scottish uplands and recreate the ancient Caledonian Forest

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Dr Razak's first patient of the day is Muriel, an elderly woman who recently suffered a mini-stroke. She is reluctant to take her blood pressure medication because she has an aversion to drugs - but as the doctor explains, not doing so could make things even worse. Elaine asks Dr Costigan to give her painful knee a once-over and 92-year-old Elizabeth comes in for her regular heart check-up

programme.title: Critical Condition
programme.desc: A mother of one suffers a ruptured brain aneurism, leaving Dr Nayak just minutes to stop the bleeding. In resus, a patient's heart stops multiple times while Dr Mandava battles to save his life. A 17-year-old girl is critically injured in a car crash. As her parents race to her side, trauma leader Dr Hall works to identify her injuries and get her to emergency surgery

programme.title: Extreme Nightmare Tenants
programme.desc: Lancashire letting agent Paul Ainsworth Lord has a run-in with a tenant while carrying out a routine inspection on a property - leading to a three-month stand-off. Harrow Council housing officers investigate a residential property being used as a brothel and a Lincolnshire pub owner calls in a lawyer when his tenant, who manages the pub, stops paying rent

programme.title: Mourinho vs Pep: The Feud
programme.desc: A look at the rivalry between Jose Mourinho and Pep Guardiola, two of the most successful football managers of the modern era. The documentary explores how the relationship soured after beginning as a friendship at Barcelona, and explores how their differing styles of football and personalities clashed in some of Europe's biggest club games. With contributions from the likes of Vincent Kompany, Gerard Pique and Eidur Gudjohnsen

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Our Dream Hotel
programme.desc: Eric and Steven have abandoned their busy London lifestyle for the relaxed atmosphere of a tiny hamlet in southern France. They have invested everything to restore and convert an 18th-century farmhouse into a luxury boutique B&B. Difficulties arise from a spiraling budget, no previous building or hospitality experience and Steven not speaking the language. With Alex Polizzi advising and scrutinising their affairs, the pressure is on to open in time for the summer season

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: Built more than 1,400 years ago, Canterbury Cathedral is both a holy place and a World Heritage site. It is the 'mother church' for more than 85 million people, and one of the biggest tourist attractions in the UK with nearly a million visitors a year. Tony tells the tale of the cathedral's founding and how, after the cathedral burnt down, it was rebuilt by the Normans as a symbol of their power. He also explores how it became wealthy thanks to its role as a major site of pilgrimage following the infamous murder of Archbishop Thomas Becket

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: A badger cub is rescued and a group of thieving squirrels gets in a tangle

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Tim Marlow examines the work of Italian Renaissance painter Raffaello Santi, known better simply as Raphael, who was born in 1483 and achieved so much in his 37-year life that he was considered equal to Leonardo da Vinci and Michelangelo. He was highly regarded for his many studies of the Madonna and Child, and artists adopted his idealised forms as the model of beauty until the end of the 19th century

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice presents a compilation of advice from past episodes, visiting an Edinburgh home suffering from a surfeit of personality, a Victorian residence in Chester that needs restoring to its former glory and a Derbyshire cottage with rather too many stuffed animals for its own good

A Staffordshire optician who owes money to a supplier claims that the equipment he was sold was faulty, while enforcement agents in north London are forced to mediate between a tenant who claims she is being evicted for having children and a landlord who says she wanted to leave in the hope of getting a council house

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Jon Bentley compares three budget-friendly coffee machines against a model worth over £15,000, while Craig Charles arms his house with a techy smart lock. Ortis Deley gains behind-the-scenes access to a company that makes drones for the British military. After taking a couple of the devices for a spin, he finds out how this top-secret tech could soon be in drones available to the public. Meanwhile, Georgie Barrat and the G Team advise a tennis coach who wants to see if gadgets can give her players the edge

programme.title: Secret Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores the Kingdom of Fife and the East, starting at Falkland Palace - a former retreat of Scottish royalty. Once a hunting lodge to eight Stuart monarchs, it is the final resting place of King James V. Susan also visits Wemyss Caves, home to the largest collection of Pictish carvings in the world, and is invited into a little-known cave whose location must remain secret. Later, she is surprised to find a Japanese garden hidden deep in the countryside and gets the chance to bat for the world's only beach cricket club on Elie beach

programme.title: Atlantic: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: In the Atlantic's far north, autumn is the season of gales, cooling temperatures and driving rain. This is the season when the wildlife must make the final preparations for winter or migrate to warmer climes. For some autumn is also the season to battle for a mate

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 2 (2017)
programme.desc: When a scheming Italian crime lord holds him to a blood oath, reluctant hitman John Wick goes on another murderous rampage. Action sequel, starring Keanu Reeves, Riccardo Scamarcio and Ian McShane

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 2 (2017)
programme.desc: When a scheming Italian crime lord holds him to a blood oath, reluctant hitman John Wick goes on another murderous rampage. Action sequel, starring Keanu Reeves, Riccardo Scamarcio and Ian McShane

programme.title: The Football Factory (2004)
programme.desc: Hard-drinking Tommy Johnson uses his love of football as an excuse for indulging in regular bouts of violence. However, the simmering conflict between fans of rival teams soon spirals out of control when Tommy is accused of raping the sister of a fellow hooligan. Drama based on John King's novel, starring Danny Dyer, Frank Harper and Neil Maskell

programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: Our Dream Hotel
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits the Bromley family, who have moved from Australia to France to restore a crumbling but magnificent 800-year-old building in Poitou-Charentes, in the hope of creating a family business for future generations

programme.title: World's Busiest Train Stations
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at Zurich Hauptbahnhof, which is the busiest railway hub in Europe and also one of the most punctual, with 90 per cent of its 3,000 trains a day running on time. In the control room, Mario has to react when a cargo of sausages spills onto the mainline, blocking commuter services and Germany-bound trains. Transport police officer Virginia heads out to respond to a late-night emergency when a brawl breaks out at a nearby station after an outdoor party is cancelled at the last moment

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice stresses the importance of first impressions, showing a couple from Portishead, Somerset, how to spruce up the exterior of their bungalow to make it look inviting. She also offers ideas on suitable colour schemes to use inside the home, demonstrating how neutral tones can create an airy, spacious atmosphere

programme.title: Dakota Incident (1956)
programme.desc: Mismatched travellers, including a bank robber, a politician and a dancing girl, are targeted by Cheyenne braves when their stagecoach is ambushed in the Dakota desert. When the coach is set on fire, they take cover in a gully, and a drawn-out conflict ensues. Western, starring Dale Robertson, Linda Darnell, John Lund and Ward Bond

programme.title: How to Murder Your Wife (1965)
programme.desc: A henpecked cartoonist publishes a comic strip depicting the murder of his new wife - who fears he is planning to bump her off for real. When she disappears without trace, he finds himself accused of murder. Comedy, starring Jack Lemmon, Virna Lisi, Terry-Thomas, Claire Trevor, Eddie Mayehoff and Sidney Blackmer

programme.title: Comanche Station (1960)
programme.desc: A cowboy recruits a trio of outlaws to rescue a woman held captive at a Comanche camp and reunite her with her husband in return for a large reward. However, his partners decide the bounty should be split three ways rather than four and plan to double-cross him. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Nancy Gates, Claude Akins and Skip Homeier

programme.title: This Happy Breed (1944)
programme.desc: A working-class family moves into a new suburban home after the father returns from service in the First World War. However, the couple and their children struggle to adjust to life higher up the social ladder. David Lean's drama, adapted by Noel Coward from his play, starring Robert Newton, Celia Johnson, Robert Stanley Holloway and John Mills

programme.title: The Madness of King George (1994)
programme.desc: British monarch George III's behaviour becomes increasingly erratic, prompting fears that he is going insane. While physicians try to uncover the cause of his condition, the king's enemies in Parliament and the frustrated Prince of Wales conspire to have him declared insane and removed from the throne. Period drama based on Alan Bennett's play, starring Nigel Hawthorne, Helen Mirren and Rupert Everett

programme.title: The Festival (2018)
programme.desc: When Nick's girlfriend dumps him at graduation, he has a colossal meltdown in front of the entire university. He's convinced his life is over, but his best mate Shane has the perfect solution - three days at a music festival. With the help of 'festival aficionado' and certified oddball Amy, Shane tries to get Nick to embrace the music, the mayhem and the mud. Comedy, starring Joe Thomas, Hannah Tointon, Hammed Animashaun and Jemaine Clement

programme.title: Rough Night (2017)
programme.desc: Things go terribly wrong for a group of girlfriends who hire a male stripper for a hen party in Miami. Comedy, starring Scarlett Johansson, Kate McKinnon and Zoe Kravitz

programme.title: Suntan (2016)
programme.desc: A middle-aged doctor moves to an island to make a fresh start, but becomes obsessed with a female patient half his age when she lets him tag along with her group of hard-partying friends. Drama, starring Makis Papadimitriou and Elli Tringou. In Greek and English

programme.title: Pony Express (1953)
programme.desc: Buffalo Bill Cody and Wild Bill Hickok work on establishing an east-west route for the pioneering mail service that will allow messages to be sent from Missouri to California in 10 days. However, they have to contend with saboteurs planning to prevent California becoming part of the Union. Western, starring Charlton Heston, Forrest Tucker, Rhonda Fleming and Jan Sterling

programme.title: A Day to Remember (1953)
programme.desc: The members of the darts team from the Hand & Flower pub in London head off on a day trip to Boulogne. Among the group is Jim, who is worried that his girlfriend plans to leave him, Shorty, a loner who hopes to join the Foreign Legion, the woman-chasing Charley, and Stan, an entrepreneur who wants to smuggle black-market watches back to the UK. Comedy, starring Stanley Holloway, Donald Sinden and Joan Rice

programme.title: Three Billboards Interview Special
programme.desc: Writer-director Martin McDonagh and star Sam Rockwell talk about making the dramatic comedy about revenge and redemption in small-town America

programme.title: A Man Alone (1955)
programme.desc: A gunman stumbles across the bodies of passengers murdered in a stagecoach robbery. Riding to the nearest town to report the crime, he finds himself accused of the killings and faces a battle to prove his innocence. Western, directed by and starring Ray Milland, with Raymond Burr, Lee Van Cleef, Mary Murphy and Ward Bond

programme.title: When Eight Bells Toll (1971)
programme.desc: A Navy intelligence agent heads to Scotland, where he poses as a marine biologist to investigate the theft of millions of pounds in gold bullion from ships off the British coast. However, the locals counter his enquiries with hostility, especially when he sets his sights on a Greek tycoon, whom he suspects may be operating as a modern-day pirate. Thriller, with Anthony Hopkins, Robert Morley and Jack Hawkins. Based on a novel by Alistair MacLean

programme.title: Now You See Me (2013)
programme.desc: Four magicians use their lavish stage shows as cover for a series of seemingly impossible heists, and give away the money they steal to their audiences. An FBI agent assembles a team of experts to discover how the baffling robberies are committed and what the secret agenda behind the crime spree is. Crime thriller, starring Jesse Eisenberg, Mark Ruffalo, Woody Harrelson and Isla Fisher

programme.title: Stargate (1994)
programme.desc: An Egyptologist and a soldier head an expedition through a mysterious portal to an alien planet. The world they find resembles ancient Egypt and is under the rule of a tyrant who has convinced the downtrodden inhabitants that he is a god - until the humans incite the enslaved populace to fight back. Sci-fi adventure, starring Kurt Russell, James Spader and Jaye Davidson

programme.title: True Grit (2010)
programme.desc: A farmer is murdered by a hired hand, who flees into the wilderness. The dead man's strong-willed 14-year-old daughter is determined to bring the killer to justice, and recruits the aid of a hard-drinking, one-eyed marshal to help her track him down. The Coen brothers' Western based on Charles Portis' novel, with Jeff Bridges, Hailee Steinfeld, Matt Damon and Josh Brolin

programme.title: Life (2015)
programme.desc: A photographer accepts an assignment to take a series of shots of rising star James Dean. They embark on a road trip together in search of locations for the images, an experience that changes the direction of the photographer's life and creates an icon. Fact-based drama, starring Robert Pattinson and Dane DeHaan

programme.title: Thunder Over Arizona (1956)
programme.desc: An honest cowboy is mistaken for the notorious outlaw he killed in self-defence. Before he can clear up the misunderstanding, he is approached by a corrupt mayor who draws him into a conspiracy to wrest control of a silver mine from its rightful owners. Western, starring Skip Homeier, Kristine Miller, George Macready and Wallace Ford

programme.title: The Dam Busters (1955)
programme.desc: The story of the destruction of the major dams in Germany's industrial heartland in the Ruhr valley in 1943, a mission that seemed impossible until engineer and scientist Barnes Wallis came up with the ingenious idea of bouncing bombs, and the pilots of 617 Squadron were trained to fly the crucial mission. Second World War drama, with Michael Redgrave, Richard Todd, Basil Sydney, Derek Farr and Ursula Jeans

programme.title: Dark Command (1940)
programme.desc: A teacher is defeated in his ambition to become the marshal of his town during the early years of the American Civil War, so he turns renegade instead. Before long he has a reputation as a feared raider fighting on the side of the South, and it falls to the newly appointed lawman whose job he coveted to stop his reign of terror. Western, starring John Wayne and Walter Pidgeon

programme.title: Carry On Teacher (1959)
programme.desc: A headmaster hopes to land a more prestigious job at a better school, but his pupils do not want him to leave. When two inspectors pay a visit, the mischievous students see a chance to sabotage his career plans with a series of pranks. Comedy, starring Ted Ray, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Kenneth Williams

programme.title: Deep Impact (1998)
programme.desc: A TV reporter investigating a presidential misdemeanour stumbles on evidence that a comet is on a collision course with Earth, threatening the future of the human race. Meanwhile, an astronaut prepares to lead his crew on a do-or-die mission to intercept the danger. Sci-fi drama, starring Robert Duvall, Tea Leoni, Morgan Freeman and Elijah Wood

programme.title: Mechanic: Resurrection (2016)
programme.desc: Hitman Arthur Bishop's arch-enemy Crain kidnaps his new girlfriend Gina and blackmails him into committing three assassinations in as many days, while making them look like accidents. Action thriller sequel, starring Jason Statham, Jessica Alba, Sam Hazeldine and Tommy Lee Jones

programme.title: Punisher: War Zone (2008)
programme.desc: The ruthless vigilante considers hanging up his guns when he learns a gangster he killed was actually an undercover FBI agent. However, he must return to his war on crime when a mobster who was hideously disfigured at his hands assembles a gang of violent criminals to cause mayhem on the streets of New York. Action thriller based on the Marvel comic-book, starring Ray Stevenson and Dominic West

programme.title: Anthropoid (2016)
programme.desc: Second World War drama based on the true story of Operation Anthropoid, the mission to assassinate SS general Reinhard Heydrich, the main architect behind the Final Solution and the Reich's third in command after Hitler and Himmler. Starring Jamie Dornan and Cillian Murphy

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Marshall's car breaks down for good just before reaching 200,000 miles, prompting the gang members to recall the adventures they had in it over the years. Comedy, starring Neil Patrick Harris and Cobie Smulders

Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey, Sean Giambrone and Hayley Orrantia

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray is honoured as one of the area's top businessmen, Beverly's attempt to help her husband find a hobby backfires, and Erica comes to the rescue when Barry's scheme to steal golf carts goes awry

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is forced to confront his fear of driving when both Penny and Howard refuse to give him a lift to work. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is flattered when a graduate student takes a shine to him, but she thinks his social life is distracting him from his work and removes him from his limited circle of friends by forcing him to work on a troublesome scientific problem. Guest starring Riki Lindhome

programme.desc: Jake and Charles take Terry on a camping trip to help him deal with stress. Amy gets Gina to help her persuade the police department to buy a product she invented as a child. Holt helps Rosa to break up with Marcus. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.desc: Jake and Amy work together on a case, but wind up arguing over Jake's old, worn-out mattress. Meanwhile, Boyle scratches Holt's prized car, and the teenage boy Rosa sponsors as a 'big brother' is in trouble with the law. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The young genius moves in with Dr Sturgis for a night to see what it would be like to leave home and go to college. During his absence, a lonely Mary tries to bond with other members of the family

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: An argument between Leonard and Sheldon over the ending of a Harry Potter book affects Amy and Penny's living arrangements. Meanwhile, Howard goes on a trip to Las Vegas with Bernadette and asks Raj to keep an eye on his mother while he is away

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Andi's doctor advises her to stop taking birth control pills, and Adam and Andi disagree on what to do. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc

programme.title: Man with a Plan

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray's furniture shop faces competition from a rival with an attention-grabbing advertising campaign, so Adam offers to make his father a television commercial starring Marvin. When Barry's attempts to court a girl at school end in failure, Beverly steps in with some advice that works a little too well

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Uncle Marvin reveals he has become a stockbroker and convinces Barry and his friends to invest their money with him. Beverly invites 50 people over for a Thanksgiving 'potluck' dinner instead of cooking for the family

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The youngster starts an internet flame war, and Missy stands up to the boys on her baseball team. Elsewhere, Meemaw is unhappy when George Sr spends time with her new boyfriend

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is offered an exciting job opportunity, but it will mean he has to leave the country for several months. Penny tries to support his ambitions, while Sheldon is jealous and attempts to discourage him from accepting the post. Meanwhile, Raja pushes the boundaries with Lucy, but puts their relationship in jeopardy

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Penny bond as they miss Leonard, and Howard tries to help Raj get over his break-up with Lucy. Meanwhile, Bernadette and Amy visit an out-of-town biology convention. Comedy about four high-achieving scientists, starring Jim Parsons, Kaley Cuoco and Johnny Galecki

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Boyle go on an eight-day stakeout, which puts a strain on their friendship. Meanwhile, Jeffords uses Santiago and Gina for inspiration when he writes a book about his daughters, and Captain Holt's nephew visits Brooklyn and makes an instant impression on Diaz. Comedy, guest starring Kyra Sedgwick and Nick Cannon

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The team visits Boyle's beach house on a detectives-only getaway, but Peralta ruins the fun by inviting Captain Holt along. programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: From entertainment juggernauts to the week's biggest news stories and from hard-hitting documentary series to gritty drama, a cast of well-known faces offers sharp, insightful, funny and sometimes emotional critiques of popular and topical TV shows, sparking debate and giving viewers the chance to share those 'we said that' moments. This episode features Denise Van Outen and her partner Eddie, Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv, Joe Swash and Stacey Solomon, Zoe Ball and her son Woody Cook, Nicola Adams and her partner Ella, Harry Redknapp and his wife Sandra, Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford, Emilia Fox and Joanna David, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Jamie Theakston and Ashley Roberts, Nigel Havers and Denis Lawson, Johnny Vegas and his PA Bev, and Jools Holland and Vic Reeves

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer

programme.title: First Dates

Maths teacher and human calculator Ian is looking for someone to help him get over his awkwardness, and hopes radiographer Rachel will be the one, while firefighter Marc meets Marilyn Monroe lookalike Elle - will it be a case of some like it hot? Do gentlemen definitely prefer blondes? Or will they turn out to be misfits?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
